topic: New Jerusalem North Pole Axis — theological axiom mapped to dome geometry
change_summary: Queue item 153. Three of four c3 holes were patched (ISS-1081, 1082, 1083 fixed): the stale "67" total and "95.2%" accuracy figure were removed from detail_evidence, the "39-domain audit engine" phrase was replaced with "live monitoring audit engine," the unlisted "4 theological claims" were enumerated as (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034), and the doubly-unfalsifiable framing was rewritten as "unfalsifiable on two independent layers ... either of which is sufficient to remove it from scientific testing." The advocate defense EXP-172 (ISS-1084) is still assigned-analyst and has not been integrated into detail_verdict_text — the defense_survives=3 attack from c3 therefore remains unmitigated in the current prose.
our_argument_summary: WIN-032 is a theological/eschatological assertion (Revelation 21 New Jerusalem placed on the dome's pole axis) with no measurable consequence, no falsification criteria, no monitoring-pipeline test, and a pole-axis location that is not in the source scripture. Counting it in the same scorecard as empirically measured quantities (Schumann, magnetic anomalies) dilutes the meaning of "CONFIRMED." Verdict: Unfalsifiable.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (line 117: "WIN-032 — New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED" with no elaboration)
raw-text/05-model.txt (line 12: "New Jerusalem descending along pole axis (WIN-032)" — one-line model-page mention)
raw-text/06-evolution.txt (line 48: "NJ axis (WIN-032)" in a list of V7+ confirmations)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (V51.0 live dashboard — 20-Domain Convergence Dashboard; WIN-032 not among the 20 monitored domains, no "jerusalem" or "032" in the file)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: None. No calculation pipeline, no live fetch, no comparison logic. The CONFIRMED label is attached as a one-line assertion in the WIN list with no computational derivation, no observed quantity, and no predicted quantity.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Not applicable — the WIN is a categorical theological label, not a numerical prediction. There is nothing to hardcode or compute. The "check" is the label itself.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None in code. In prose, the dome's framing ("CONFIRMED" alongside a pole-axis reading of Revelation 21) is inherently immune to falsification — a future world in which the New Jerusalem does not descend on the pole could still be accommodated as "not yet."
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our claims that monitor.py excludes WIN-032 and that the live dashboard does not test it are confirmed by the current raw-text archive (grep for "jerusalem\|032" in 08-live-power.txt returns zero matches; the 20-domain dashboard structure does not include it).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No domain, no URL fetch, no comparison for WIN-032 anywhere in the dome's monitoring pipeline. raw-text/08-live-power.txt enumerates 20 monitored domains (Schumann, NMP drift, equatorial gravity, eclipse EM, GPS Sagnac, etc.); none are the New Jerusalem axis. grep for "jerusalem\|032" in 08-live-power.txt returns zero matches. The claim appears in static WIN-list text (02-wins.txt) and a one-line model mention (05-model.txt), never in executable code.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics has no claim about the New Jerusalem; this is not a physics phenomenon the dome is renaming. It is a theological assertion with no physics analog.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Revelation 21 predates the dome model by ~1,900 years. The dome model adopts the text and assigns a pole-axis location that is not in the source (Revelation 21:2 describes the city as "coming down out of heaven from God" with no axis specified; 21:16 gives dimensions of a four-square city of 12,000 stadia per side but no geographic coordinate). The dome's contribution is the pole-axis mapping, which is itself not a prediction — it is a re-reading.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: There is no derivation from dome parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, moon_altitude) to the pole-axis location of a prophetic city. The dome's single symmetry axis coincides with the north pole by construction, and the pole-axis assignment is a theological/aesthetic choice, not a numerical derivation.

holes_found.description: Advocate defense from c3 (defense_survives=3) still unmitigated in the current prose. The strongest dome-side move — "you reject the wrapper and pretend you have refuted the content; we never offered WIN-032 as a measurement, the CONFIRMED label applies in our framework because the prophecy is consistent with dome geometry" — is not named or pre-empted in either detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text. The preemptive_recommendation from c3 (insist on one definition of CONFIRMED across the scorecard, with the Schumann/magnetic contrast made explicit) has not been added. EXP-172 (ISS-1084) is still "assigned-analyst" and has not been integrated. Until it is, a careful defender can reasonably argue we have critiqued the LABEL without engaging the DOME'S OWN DEFINITION of that label.
holes_found.recommendation: Wait for EXP-172; once analyst writes the neutralization, fold 2–3 sentences into detail_verdict_text that explicitly name the "two definitions of CONFIRMED" move and refuse it. Something like: "The dome author may stipulate that, within his framework, CONFIRMED means 'consistent with the theological wrapper' rather than 'measurement matched prediction to threshold.' He is free to do so — but the two definitions cannot coexist in the same scorecard. Either CONFIRMED has a single meaning across all WINs (in which case WIN-032 does not qualify, because it has no measurement), or the scorecard must be split (empirical vs. theological). Mixing the two and quoting a single accuracy percentage is the category error; our argument is not against the theology but against the label."

holes_found.description: Hardcoded count "4 theological claims in the current WIN count (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034)" violates the project's own "never hardcode a number derivable from data" rule. If an additional WIN is tagged Unfalsifiable+theological, or if one of these four is re-classified (e.g., WIN-023 or WIN-031 moves to "Not Demonstrated"), this sentence silently goes stale. The explicit ID list compounds the drift risk.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace with a rendering hook that counts Unfalsifiable WINs at build time from wins.json (the same mechanism sections.json uses for scorecard counts), or at minimum add a BUILD: check that the WIN-IDs listed here correspond to all current verdict=Unfalsifiable entries. Alternative cheap fix: pluralize more carefully ("a small cluster of theological claims, currently four — see the Unfalsifiable-verdict rows in the WIN table") so the number is anchored to a visible tally rather than a floating integer.

holes_found.description: Internal duplication inside detail_evidence: "The dome's own automated monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include WIN-032 in its live monitoring audit engine — if the author considered this a genuine, testable prediction, it would appear in the monitoring pipeline. Its complete absence from the code is a tacit admission that no computational verification is possible for a theological axiom." is restated in the same block as "The model's own monitoring system (monitor.py) contains no validation domain for WIN-032, confirming that 'CONFIRMED' is self-awarded rather than computationally verified." These are the same argument written twice. The c3 duplicate-between-evidence-and-verdict issue was resolved (detail_verdict_text now mentions monitor.py only once), but the internal repetition inside detail_evidence was not.
holes_found.recommendation: Delete the second sentence ("The model's own monitoring system...") or merge the two into one sharper statement. The first version is stronger; the second is a softer restatement.

holes_found.description: The new framing "unfalsifiable on two independent layers — the dome geometry and the prophetic framework — either of which is sufficient to remove it from scientific testing" is logically stronger than the c3 "doubly unfalsifiable ... requires both," but a careful defender can push back: the dome's geometry as such (a flat disc with a single symmetry axis) is not intrinsically unfalsifiable — it is falsifiable by many of our own arguments elsewhere in the review. What is unfalsifiable is the MAPPING of a prophetic event onto that axis. Calling the geometry itself an independent unfalsifiable layer concedes too much (it implies the dome is also unfalsifiable as a physical claim) and is also in tension with the rest of our review.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the phrase: "unfalsifiable on two independent layers — the prophetic framework (no measurable consequence, no timeline) and the pole-axis mapping (not present in the source text, added by the dome author)." Keeps the "two layers, either sufficient" structure without the collateral implication that the dome geometry itself is unfalsifiable.

holes_found.description: The phrase "the geopolitical framing in context.html confirms this is theological-political assertion, not testable physics" in detail_verdict_text is under-supported. The reader has to trust us that context.html has "geopolitical framing" — no quote, no anchor, no citation to a specific passage. A defender can and will ask: which framing, what page, what sentence?
holes_found.recommendation: Either cite the specific sentence from raw-text/07-ai-context.txt with an anchor, or drop the clause. The argument does not need the geopolitical framing — the theological/unfalsifiable basis is already sufficient. Leaving it unsupported weakens a point that is otherwise already made.

stronger_arguments.description: The "CONFIRMED" scorecard line (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 117) is only ten words: "WIN-032 — New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED." There is no finding, no date, no measurement, no falsification threshold, no method. Every other CONFIRMED WIN in the 02-wins.txt list (including the prospective-weekly WIN-035 through WIN-039) carries at least a parenthetical data value. The bare label is itself evidence that the dome author knows this is categorically different from the numerical WINs and has nonetheless chosen to place it in the same list. Quoting the exact ten-word line is a clean, short, devastating exhibit.

Revelation 21 is short — two chapters — and easy to quote in full. Verse 21:2 says: "And I John saw the holy city, new Jerusalem, coming down from God out of heaven, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband." Verse 21:16 says: "And the city lieth foursquare, and the length is as large as the breadth: and he measured the city with the reed, twelve thousand furlongs. The length and the breadth and the height of it are equal." No pole. No axis. No latitude. The dome author has added the pole-axis location. Surfacing the KJV text verbatim and daring the reader to find the axis in it is the cleanest way to establish post-hoc insertion.

The dome's live dashboard (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) is an accidental admission. The dome author decided, when building his own audit infrastructure, which WINs to test and which to exclude. He included 20 present-tense measurable domains (Schumann, NMP drift, gravity, eclipse EM, etc.). He excluded WIN-032. That exclusion is an operational act, not a rhetorical one — it is the dome author's own classifier telling us which WINs are physics and which are not. Our review should name this explicitly: "By his own hand, the dome author has already separated the testable from the untestable; we are simply reading his own classifier back to him."

kernel_of_truth.description: Integrating cosmology with prophetic texts is a coherent intellectual project in theology (medieval cathedral architecture encoded cosmic order; modern process theology attempts similar integration). Identifying the dome's single symmetry axis as the privileged locus for eschatological events is internally consistent with the dome's architecture — if you have exactly one symmetry axis and a tradition that places the throne of God at the apex, mapping the descent of the New Jerusalem along that axis is the natural move. It is not ad hoc within the framework.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Internal consistency is necessary but not sufficient. A claim can be internally coherent and still fail empirical confirmation: (1) there is no measurement that could distinguish worlds where WIN-032 is true from worlds where it is false; (2) the claim is future-tensed with no specified timeline; (3) the pole-axis specification is not in Revelation but is the dome author's addition; (4) the dome's own monitoring pipeline excludes it. Labeling an internally-consistent theological claim CONFIRMED alongside measured magnetic anomalies is a category error, not a validated prediction. The kernel of truth lives inside the framework; the scorecard inflation happens at the boundary between the framework and the external world.

advocate_mode.best_defense: You have not refuted WIN-032; you have refused it. Your review assumes that a WIN only counts if it meets the same criteria as a Schumann-resonance measurement — a falsifiable numerical prediction with a pre-registered threshold. But the dome model is explicit about integrating physics with prophetic texts (raw-text/05-model.txt line 12, raw-text/06-evolution.txt line 48); WIN-032 was never offered as a measurement. CONFIRMED, within our framework, means "consistent with dome geometry." You are applying a standard from outside the framework and then declaring the claim fails that external standard. That is a category disagreement, not a demonstration of error. If you reject the framework, say so — but do not pretend you have shown the claim to be wrong on its own terms.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: This is the c3 defense_survives=3 position, now at 2 because the revised detail_verdict_text explicitly frames the scorecard-inflation argument as being about the CONFIRMED LABEL rather than about the theology itself ("Counting future supernatural events as confirmed predictions inflates the model's scorecard" plus the post-hoc paragraph). The move from 3 to 2 is partial credit for the patched prose: we are closer to naming the category-error move. But the defense still survives because we have not explicitly refused the "two meanings of CONFIRMED" gambit in the prose itself. EXP-172 is meant to do exactly this. Once EXP-172 is integrated with a passage like "The dome author may define CONFIRMED as 'consistent with the theological wrapper' — but that definition cannot share a scorecard with Schumann-resonance measurements and magnetic-field anomalies that have explicit falsification thresholds; mixing the two is the category error," defense_survives should drop to 1.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: All four currently Unfalsifiable WINs (023 SAA-950AD/Satan's-little-season, 031 North Pole Cosmic Mountain, 032 New Jerusalem pole axis, 034 Firmament=copper/bronze) share the same adversarial architecture: absence from monitor.py, post-hoc retrofitting of ancient text to dome geometry, and counting in the scorecard-inflation pattern. Our arguments across the four are compatible and mutually reinforcing. The c3 minor rhetorical inconsistency (WIN-032 called "doubly unfalsifiable" while WIN-031 treated unfalsifiability as binary) is now resolved — WIN-032's current "two independent layers" framing is neutral about whether it is "more unfalsifiable" than WIN-031; it simply enumerates the layers. No cross-WIN contradiction remains. However, the "hardcoded list of four WIN IDs" issue flagged above is a cross-WIN coupling: if WIN-023's verdict flips (it has been questioned in prior curmudgeon reviews for potentially being Refuted/Self-Contradicted rather than Unfalsifiable), WIN-032's prose silently breaks. That is a structural fragility, not a rhetorical one.

claims_checked: "4 theological claims in the current WIN count (WIN-023, WIN-031, WIN-032, WIN-034)" — verified. Scanning wins.json for verdict==="Unfalsifiable" yields exactly 4 WINs with IDs 023, 031, 032, 034. The count and the list are correct as of 2026-04-17.

"The dome's own automated monitoring system (monitor.py) does not include WIN-032 in its live monitoring audit engine" — verified. raw-text/08-live-power.txt (the V51.0 live dashboard archive) enumerates 20 monitored domains; grep for "jerusalem\|032" in that file returns zero matches. monitor.py is the dome's own automation per raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 161 ("GitHub Actions runs monitor.py every 5 minutes, fetching from NOAA, USGS, INTERMAGNET").
"The dome site classifies this as a 'CONFIRMED' prediction" — verified. raw-text/02-wins.txt line 117: "WIN-032 — New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED". Exact string match.

"the pole-axis location is not in the source text (Revelation 21)" — verified by inspection of Revelation 21:2 and 21:16; no pole, no axis, no latitude, no coordinate specified in the canonical text. The claim that the dome author added the pole-axis location is accurate.
Verdict-level classification: monitoring=none, relabels_standard=false, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false — all four tags match the dome code analysis above.
quantitative_verification.computations: Ran: node -e "const w=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('data/wins.json','utf8'));console.log(Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(w.reduce((a,x)=>(a[x.verdict]=(a[x.verdict]||0)+1,a),{}))));" → Unfalsifiable:4. Ran grep -n -i "jerusalem\|032" raw-text/08-live-power.txt → no matches. Read raw-text/02-wins.txt line 117 directly. Inspected Revelation 21 (KJV, public-domain text) for pole/axis references → none present.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None. All quantitative claims in current detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text check out against the fresh data. This is a substantial improvement over c3, which flagged three stale numbers (67, 95.2%, 39-domain). The decider's patches correctly resolved those.

deeper_analysis: WIN-032 is now in a narrow but stable state: the numerical-staleness problems from c3 are fixed, the cross-WIN consistency is clean, and the verdict (Unfalsifiable) is well-supported. The remaining work is rhetorical rather than factual: explicitly refuse the "two definitions of CONFIRMED" move that is the dome's strongest defense, and tighten a few phrases where the current prose still concedes more ground than needed. This is the kind of item where the review is correct but could be sharper. Nothing here threatens the verdict; everything here is about making the argument bulletproof against a careful theological/philosophical defender. The critical watch-items are (a) EXP-172 integration and (b) the hardcoded "4 + ID list" drift risk. Once EXP-172 is integrated and the count is computed at build time, this WIN is effectively done and can drop to spot-check cadence.

citations_verified: raw-text/02-wins.txt line 117 (WIN-032 — New Jerusalem North Pole Axis CONFIRMED) — exact match
raw-text/05-model.txt line 12 (New Jerusalem descending along pole axis (WIN-032)) — exact match
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (V51.0 20-Domain Convergence Dashboard; no WIN-032 / "jerusalem" / "032") — verified by grep
raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 161 (monitor.py automation) — verified, used to anchor monitor.py claim
citations_unchecked: The detail_verdict_text reference to "geopolitical framing in context.html" — not tied to a specific line; flagged as a minor hole above. No external citation failed, but the internal reference is under-specified.
The "theological assertion" language does not cite Revelation 21 directly in detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text — stronger_arguments suggests adding the verse numbers.
